q1_sink_dangerous.evidence: Sink is HttpResponse(...) which only constructs a response object in memory; no network I/O, shell execution, or irreversible side effects.

q2_observable_after_return.evidence: Reflective XSS violation is observable by inspecting the content of the returned HttpResponse object for unsanitized user input.

q3_result_in_memory.evidence: returns.kind is 'value' and returns.exprs include HttpResponse objects, meaning the response content can be inspected directly in memory on return.
